{"step_by_step_thinking": "Plaque is described in the document from MEDCORP (Source: MEDCORP, Document [28]) as a 'biofilm of various bacteria (mainly Streptococcus and anaerobes)' that forms on the surface of teeth. This directly indicates that the primary component of plaque is microorganisms. While minerals (like calcium) can contribute to mineralization of plaque and form tartar, they are not the primary or most numerous component. Food debris may be present in the initial stages, but it is not the defining or most numerous component. Leucocytes (white blood cells) are part of the host's immune response to plaque and are present in response to infection, but they are not the main structural component of plaque. The term 'biofilm' emphasizes the presence of microorganisms as the foundational element. Therefore, microorganisms are the most numerous component of plaque.", "answer_choice": "C"}
